### You Should Associate (Numbered Discourses 10.155) ^an10-155-0

“Mendicants, you should not associate with an individual who has ten qualities. ^an10-155-1-1

What ten? ^an10-155-1-2

Wrong view, wrong purpose, wrong speech, wrong action, wrong livelihood, wrong effort, wrong mindfulness, wrong immersion, wrong knowledge, and wrong freedom. ^an10-155-1-3

You should not associate with an individual who has these ten qualities. ^an10-155-1-4

You should associate with an individual who has ten qualities. ^an10-155-2-1

What ten? ^an10-155-2-2

Right view, right purpose, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, right immersion, right knowledge, and right freedom. ^an10-155-2-3

You should associate with an individual who has these ten qualities.” ^an10-155-2-4

### Frequenting, Etc. (Numbered Discourses 10.156–166) ^an10-156-166-0

“Mendicants, you should not frequent an individual who has ten qualities. … ^an10-156-166-1-1

you should frequent … ^an10-156-166-1-2

you should not pay homage … ^an10-156-166-1-3

you should pay homage … ^an10-156-166-1-4

you should not venerate … ^an10-156-166-1-5

you should venerate … ^an10-156-166-1-6

you should not praise … ^an10-156-166-1-7

you should praise … ^an10-156-166-1-8

you should not respect … ^an10-156-166-1-9

you should respect … ^an10-156-166-1-10

you should not revere … ^an10-156-166-1-11

you should revere … ^an10-156-166-1-12

is not a success … ^an10-156-166-1-13

is a success … ^an10-156-166-1-14

is not pure … ^an10-156-166-1-15

is pure … ^an10-156-166-1-16

does not win over conceit … ^an10-156-166-1-17

wins over conceit … ^an10-156-166-1-18

does not grow in wisdom … ^an10-156-166-1-19

grows in wisdom … ^an10-156-166-1-20

brims with much wickedness … ^an10-156-166-2-1

brims with much merit. ^an10-156-166-2-2

What ten? ^an10-156-166-2-3

Right view, right purpose, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, right immersion, right knowledge, and right freedom. ^an10-156-166-2-4

An individual who has these ten qualities creates much merit.” ^an10-156-166-2-5

### The Brahmin Ceremony of Descent (Numbered Discourses 10.167) ^an10-167-0

Now, at that time it was the sabbath. The brahmin Jānussoṇi had bathed his head and dressed in a new pair of linen robes. Holding a handful of fresh grass, he stood to one side not far from the Buddha. ^an10-167-1-1

The Buddha saw him, ^an10-167-2-1

and said, ^an10-167-2-2

“Brahmin, why have you bathed your head and dressed in a new pair of linen robes? Why are you standing to one side holding a handful of fresh grass? ^an10-167-2-3

What’s going on today with the brahmin clan?” ^an10-167-2-4

“Worthy Gotama, today is the ceremony of descent for the brahmin clan.” ^an10-167-2-5

“But how do the brahmins observe the ceremony of descent?” ^an10-167-3-1

“Well, worthy Gotama, on the sabbath the brahmins bathe their heads and dress in a new pair of linen robes. They make a heap of fresh cow dung and spread it with green grass. Then they make their beds between the boundary and the fire chamber. ^an10-167-3-2

That night they rise three times and worship the fire with cupped palms: ^an10-167-3-3

‘We descend, lord! We descend, lord!’ ^an10-167-3-4

And they serve the fire with abundant ghee, oil, and butter. ^an10-167-3-5

And when the night has passed they serve the brahmins with delicious fresh and cooked foods. ^an10-167-3-6

That’s how the brahmins observe the ceremony of descent.” ^an10-167-3-7

“The ceremony of descent observed by the brahmins is quite different from that observed in the training of the Noble One.” ^an10-167-4-1

“But worthy Gotama, how is the ceremony of descent observed in the training of the Noble One? ^an10-167-4-2

Worthy Gotama, please teach me this.” ^an10-167-4-3

“Well then, brahmin, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.” ^an10-167-5-1

“Yes, worthy sir,” Jānussoṇi replied. ^an10-167-5-2

The Buddha said this: ^an10-167-5-3

“It’s when a noble disciple reflects: ^an10-167-6-1

‘Killing living creatures has a bad result in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-167-6-2

Reflecting like this, they give up killing living creatures, ^an10-167-6-3

they descend from killing living creatures. ^an10-167-6-4

… ‘Stealing has a bad result ^an10-167-7-1

in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-167-7-2

Reflecting like this, they give up stealing, ^an10-167-7-3

they descend from stealing. ^an10-167-7-4

… ‘Sexual misconduct has a bad result ^an10-167-8-1

in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-167-8-2

Reflecting like this, they give up sexual misconduct, ^an10-167-8-3

they descend from sexual misconduct. ^an10-167-8-4

… ‘Lying has a bad result ^an10-167-9-1

in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-167-9-2

Reflecting like this, they give up lying, ^an10-167-9-3

they descend from lying. ^an10-167-9-4

… ‘Backbiting has a bad result ^an10-167-10-1

in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-167-10-2

Reflecting like this, they give up backbiting, ^an10-167-10-3

they descend from backbiting. ^an10-167-10-4

… ‘Harsh speech has a bad result ^an10-167-11-1

in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-167-11-2

Reflecting like this, they give up harsh speech, ^an10-167-11-3

they descend from harsh speech. ^an10-167-11-4

… ‘Talking nonsense has a bad result ^an10-167-12-1

in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-167-12-2

Reflecting like this, they give up talking nonsense, ^an10-167-12-3

they descend from talking nonsense. ^an10-167-12-4

… ‘Covetousness has a bad result ^an10-167-13-1

in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-167-13-2

Reflecting like this, they give up covetousness, ^an10-167-13-3

they descend from covetousness. ^an10-167-13-4

… ‘Ill will has a bad result ^an10-167-14-1

in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-167-14-2

Reflecting like this, they give up ill will, ^an10-167-14-3

they descend from ill will. ^an10-167-14-4

‘Wrong view has a bad result ^an10-167-15-1

in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-167-15-2

Reflecting like this, they give up wrong view, ^an10-167-15-3

they descend from wrong view. ^an10-167-15-4

This is the ceremony of descent in the training of the Noble One.” ^an10-167-15-5

“The ceremony of descent observed by the brahmins is quite different from that observed in the training of the Noble One. ^an10-167-16-1

And, worthy Gotama, the ceremony of descent observed by the brahmins is not worth a sixteenth part of the ceremony of descent observed in the training of the Noble One. ^an10-167-16-2

Excellent, worthy Gotama, excellent! … ^an10-167-16-3

From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.” ^an10-167-16-4

### The Noble Descent (Numbered Discourses 10.168) ^an10-168-0

“Mendicants, I will teach you the noble descent. ^an10-168-1-1

Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.” ^an10-168-1-2

“Yes, sir,” they replied. ^an10-168-1-3

The Buddha said this: ^an10-168-1-4

“And what, mendicants, is the noble descent? ^an10-168-2-1

It’s when a noble disciple reflects: ^an10-168-2-2

‘Killing living creatures has a bad result ^an10-168-2-3

in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-168-2-4

Reflecting like this, they give up killing living creatures, ^an10-168-2-5

they descend from killing living creatures. ^an10-168-2-6

… ‘Stealing has a bad result ^an10-168-3-1

in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-168-3-2

Reflecting like this, they give up stealing, ^an10-168-3-3

they descend from stealing. ^an10-168-3-4

… ‘Sexual misconduct has a bad result …’ … ^an10-168-4-1

they descend from sexual misconduct. ^an10-168-4-2

… ‘Lying has a bad result …’ … ^an10-168-5-1

they descend from lying. ^an10-168-5-2

… ‘Backbiting has a bad result …’ … ^an10-168-6-1

they descend from backbiting. ^an10-168-6-2

… ‘Harsh speech has a bad result …’ … ^an10-168-7-1

they descend from harsh speech. ^an10-168-7-2

… ‘Talking nonsense has a bad result …’ … ^an10-168-8-1

they descend from talking nonsense. ^an10-168-8-2

… ‘Covetousness has a bad result …’ … ^an10-168-9-1

they descend from covetousness. ^an10-168-9-2

… ‘Ill will has a bad result …’ … ^an10-168-10-1

they descend from ill will. ^an10-168-10-2

… ‘Wrong view has a bad result both in the present life and in lives to come.’ ^an10-168-11-1

Reflecting like this, they give up wrong view, ^an10-168-11-2

they descend from wrong view. ^an10-168-11-3

This is called the noble descent.” ^an10-168-11-4

### With Saṅgārava (Numbered Discourses 10.169) ^an10-169-0

Then Saṅgārava the brahmin went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him. ^an10-169-1-1

When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha: ^an10-169-1-2

“Worthy Gotama, what is the near shore? And what is the far shore?” ^an10-169-2-1

“Killing living creatures is the near shore, brahmin, and not killing living creatures is the far shore. ^an10-169-2-2

Stealing is the near shore, and not stealing is the far shore. ^an10-169-2-3

Sexual misconduct is the near shore, and avoiding sexual misconduct is the far shore. ^an10-169-2-4

Lying is the near shore, and not lying is the far shore. ^an10-169-2-5

Backbiting is the near shore, and avoiding backbiting is the far shore. ^an10-169-2-6

Harsh speech is the near shore, and avoiding harsh speech is the far shore. ^an10-169-2-7

Talking nonsense is the near shore, and avoiding talking nonsense is the far shore. ^an10-169-2-8

Covetousness is the near shore, and contentment is the far shore. ^an10-169-2-9

Ill will is the near shore, and good will is the far shore. ^an10-169-2-10

Wrong view is the near shore, and right view is the far shore. ^an10-169-2-11

This is the near shore, and this is the far shore. ^an10-169-2-12

Few are those among humans ^an10-169-3-1

who cross to the far shore. ^an10-169-3-2

The rest just run ^an10-169-3-3

around on the near shore. ^an10-169-3-4

When the teaching is well explained, ^an10-169-4-1

those who practice accordingly ^an10-169-4-2

are the ones who will cross over ^an10-169-4-3

Death’s dominion so hard to pass. ^an10-169-4-4

Rid of dark qualities, ^an10-169-5-1

an astute person should develop the bright. ^an10-169-5-2

Leaving home behind ^an10-169-5-3

for the seclusion so hard to enjoy, ^an10-169-5-4

try to find satisfaction there, ^an10-169-6-1

having left behind sensual pleasures. ^an10-169-6-2

Owning nothing, an astute person ^an10-169-6-3

should cleanse themselves of mental corruptions. ^an10-169-6-4

And those whose minds are rightly developed ^an10-169-7-1

in the awakening factors; ^an10-169-7-2

letting go of attachments, ^an10-169-7-3

they delight in not grasping. ^an10-169-7-4

With defilements ended, brilliant, ^an10-169-7-5

they are quenched in this world.” ^an10-169-7-6

### The Near Shore (Numbered Discourses 10.170) ^an10-170-0

“Mendicants, I will teach you the near shore and the far shore. ^an10-170-1-1

Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak. … ^an10-170-1-2

And what, mendicants, is the near shore? What is the far shore? ^an10-170-1-3

Killing living creatures is the near shore, mendicants, and not killing living creatures is the far shore. ^an10-170-1-4

Stealing is the near shore, and not stealing is the far shore. ^an10-170-1-5

Sexual misconduct is the near shore, and avoiding sexual misconduct is the far shore. ^an10-170-1-6

Lying is the near shore, and not lying is the far shore. ^an10-170-1-7

Backbiting is the near shore, and avoiding backbiting is the far shore. ^an10-170-1-8

Harsh speech is the near shore, and avoiding harsh speech is the far shore. ^an10-170-1-9

Talking nonsense is the near shore, and avoiding talking nonsense is the far shore. ^an10-170-1-10

Covetousness is the near shore, and contentment is the far shore. ^an10-170-1-11

Ill will is the near shore, and good will is the far shore. ^an10-170-1-12

Wrong view is the near shore, and right view is the far shore. ^an10-170-1-13

This is the near shore, and this is the far shore. ^an10-170-1-14

Few are those among humans ^an10-170-2-1

who cross to the far shore. ^an10-170-2-2

The rest just run ^an10-170-2-3

around on the near shore. ^an10-170-2-4

When the teaching is well explained, ^an10-170-3-1

those who practice accordingly ^an10-170-3-2

are the ones who will cross over ^an10-170-3-3

Death’s dominion so hard to pass. ^an10-170-3-4

Rid of dark qualities, ^an10-170-4-1

an astute person should develop the bright. ^an10-170-4-2

Leaving home behind ^an10-170-4-3

for the seclusion so hard to enjoy, ^an10-170-4-4

try to find satisfaction there, ^an10-170-5-1

having left behind sensual pleasures. ^an10-170-5-2

Owning nothing, an astute person ^an10-170-5-3

should cleanse themselves of mental corruptions. ^an10-170-5-4

And those whose minds are rightly developed ^an10-170-6-1

in the awakening factors; ^an10-170-6-2

letting go of attachments, ^an10-170-6-3

they delight in not grasping. ^an10-170-6-4

With defilements ended, brilliant, ^an10-170-6-5

they are quenched in this world.” ^an10-170-6-6

### Bad Principles (1st) (Numbered Discourses 10.171) ^an10-171-0

“Mendicants, you should know bad principles with bad results. ^an10-171-1-1

And you should know good principles with good results. ^an10-171-1-2

Knowing these things, your practice should follow the good principles with good results. ^an10-171-1-3

And what are bad principles with bad results? ^an10-171-2-1

Killing living creatures, stealing, and sexual misconduct; speech that’s false, backbiting, harsh, or nonsensical; covetousness, ill will, and wrong view. ^an10-171-2-2

These are called bad principles with bad results. ^an10-171-2-3

And what are good principles with good results? ^an10-171-3-1

Avoiding killing living creatures, stealing, and sexual misconduct; avoiding speech that’s false, backbiting, harsh, or nonsensical; contentment, good will, and right view. ^an10-171-3-2

These are called good principles with good results. ^an10-171-3-3

‘You should know bad principles with bad results. ^an10-171-4-1

And you should know good principles with good results. ^an10-171-4-2

Knowing these things, your practice should follow the good principles with good results.’ ^an10-171-4-3

That’s what I said, and this is why I said it.” ^an10-171-4-4

### Bad Principles (2nd) (Numbered Discourses 10.172) ^an10-172-0

“Mendicants, you should know bad principles and good principles. ^an10-172-1-1

And you should know bad results and good results. ^an10-172-1-2

Knowing these things, your practice should follow the good principles with good results.” ^an10-172-1-3

That is what the Buddha said. ^an10-172-1-4

When he had spoken, the Holy One rose from his seat and entered his dwelling. ^an10-172-1-5

Soon after the Buddha left, those mendicants considered, ^an10-172-2-1

“The Buddha gave this brief summary recital, then entered his dwelling without explaining the meaning in detail. ^an10-172-2-2

‘You should know bad principles and good principles. ^an10-172-2-3

And you should know bad results and good results. ^an10-172-2-4

Knowing these things, your practice should follow the good principles with good results.’ ^an10-172-2-5

Who can explain in detail the meaning of this brief summary recital given by the Buddha?” ^an10-172-2-6

Then those mendicants thought, ^an10-172-3-1

“This Venerable Mahākaccāna is praised by the Buddha and esteemed by his sensible spiritual companions. ^an10-172-3-2

He is capable of explaining in detail the meaning of this brief summary recital given by the Buddha. ^an10-172-3-3

Let’s go to him, and ask him about this matter. ^an10-172-3-4

As he answers, so we’ll remember it.” ^an10-172-3-5

Then those mendicants went to Mahākaccāna, and exchanged greetings with him. ^an10-172-4-1

When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side. They told him what had happened, and said, ^an10-172-4-2

“May Venerable Mahākaccāna please explain this.” ^an10-172-7-6

“Reverends, suppose there was a person in need of heartwood. And while wandering in search of heartwood he’d come across a large tree standing with heartwood. But he’d pass over the roots and trunk, imagining that the heartwood should be sought in the branches and leaves. ^an10-172-8-1

Such is the consequence for the venerables. Though you were face to face with the Buddha, you bypassed him, imagining that you should ask me about this matter. ^an10-172-8-2

For he is the Buddha, the one who knows and sees. He is vision, he is knowledge, he is the manifestation of principle, he is the manifestation of divinity. He is the teacher, the proclaimer, the elucidator of meaning, the bestower of freedom from death, the lord of truth, the Realized One. ^an10-172-8-3

That was the time to approach the Buddha and ask about this matter. ^an10-172-8-4

You should have remembered it in line with the Buddha’s answer.” ^an10-172-8-5

“Certainly he is the Buddha, the one who knows and sees. He is vision, he is knowledge, he is the manifestation of principle, he is the manifestation of divinity. He is the teacher, the proclaimer, the elucidator of meaning, the bestower of freedom from death, the lord of truth, the Realized One. ^an10-172-9-1

That was the time to approach the Buddha and ask about this matter. ^an10-172-9-2

We should have remembered it in line with the Buddha’s answer. ^an10-172-9-3

Still, Venerable Mahākaccāna is praised by the Buddha and esteemed by his sensible spiritual companions. ^an10-172-9-4

He is capable of explaining in detail the meaning of this brief summary recital given by the Buddha. ^an10-172-9-5

Please explain this, if it’s no trouble.” ^an10-172-9-6

“Well then, reverends, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.” ^an10-172-10-1

“Yes, reverend,” they replied. ^an10-172-10-2

Mahākaccāna said this: ^an10-172-10-3

“Reverends, the Buddha gave this brief summary recital, then entered his dwelling without explaining the meaning in detail: ^an10-172-11-1

‘You should know bad principles and good principles … ^an10-172-11-2

and practice accordingly.’ ^an10-172-11-3

So what are bad principles? What are good principles? ^an10-172-12-1

What are bad results? And what are good results? ^an10-172-12-2

Killing living creatures is a bad principle. ^an10-172-12-3

Not killing living creatures is a good principle. ^an10-172-12-4

And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by killing living creatures are bad results. ^an10-172-12-5

And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of not killing living creatures are good results. ^an10-172-12-6

Stealing is a bad principle. ^an10-172-13-1

Not stealing is a good principle. ^an10-172-13-2

And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by stealing are bad results. ^an10-172-13-3

And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of not stealing are good results. ^an10-172-13-4

Sexual misconduct is a bad principle. ^an10-172-14-1

Avoiding sexual misconduct is a good principle. ^an10-172-14-2

And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by sexual misconduct are bad results. ^an10-172-14-3

And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of avoiding sexual misconduct are good results. ^an10-172-14-4

Lying is a bad principle. ^an10-172-15-1

Not lying is a good principle. ^an10-172-15-2

And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by lying are bad results. ^an10-172-15-3

And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of not lying are good results. ^an10-172-15-4

Backbiting is a bad principle. ^an10-172-16-1

Avoiding backbiting is a good principle. ^an10-172-16-2

And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by backbiting are bad results. ^an10-172-16-3

And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of avoiding backbiting are good results. ^an10-172-16-4

Harsh speech is a bad principle. ^an10-172-17-1

Avoiding harsh speech is a good principle. ^an10-172-17-2

And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by harsh speech are bad results. ^an10-172-17-3

And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of avoiding harsh speech are good results. ^an10-172-17-4

Talking nonsense is a bad principle. ^an10-172-18-1

Avoiding talking nonsense is a good principle. ^an10-172-18-2

And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by talking nonsense are bad results. ^an10-172-18-3

And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of avoiding talking nonsense are good results. ^an10-172-18-4

Covetousness is a bad principle. ^an10-172-19-1

Contentment is a good principle. ^an10-172-19-2

And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by covetousness are bad results. ^an10-172-19-3

And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of contentment are good results. ^an10-172-19-4

Ill will is a bad principle. ^an10-172-20-1

Good will is a good principle. ^an10-172-20-2

And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by ill will are bad results. ^an10-172-20-3

And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of good will are good results. ^an10-172-20-4

Wrong view is a bad principle. ^an10-172-21-1

Right view is a good principle. ^an10-172-21-2

And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by wrong view are bad results. ^an10-172-21-3

And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of right view are good results. ^an10-172-21-4

The Buddha gave this brief summary recital, then entered his dwelling without explaining the meaning in detail: ^an10-172-22-1

‘You should know bad principles and good principles … ^an10-172-22-2

and practice accordingly.’ ^an10-172-22-3

And this is how I understand the detailed meaning of this summary recital. ^an10-172-22-4

If you wish, you may go to the Buddha and ask him about this. ^an10-172-22-5

You should remember it in line with the Buddha’s answer.” ^an10-172-22-6

“Yes, reverend,” said those mendicants, approving and agreeing with what Mahākaccāna said. Then they rose from their seats and went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened. Then they said: ^an10-172-23-1

“Sir, we went to Mahākaccāna and asked him about this matter. ^an10-172-27-1

And Mahākaccāna clearly explained the meaning to us in this manner, with these words and phrases.” ^an10-172-27-2

“Good, good, mendicants! ^an10-172-28-1

Mahākaccāna is astute, he has great wisdom. ^an10-172-28-2

If you came to me and asked this question, I would answer it in exactly the same way as Mahākaccāna. ^an10-172-28-3

That is what it means, and that’s how you should remember it.” ^an10-172-28-4

### Bad Principles (3rd) (Numbered Discourses 10.173) ^an10-173-0

“Mendicants, you should know bad principles and good principles. ^an10-173-1-1

And you should know bad results and good results. ^an10-173-1-2

Knowing these things, your practice should follow the good principles with good results. ^an10-173-1-3

So what are bad principles? What are good principles? What are bad results? And what are good results? ^an10-173-2-1

Killing living creatures is a bad principle. ^an10-173-2-3

Not killing living creatures is a good principle. ^an10-173-2-4

And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by killing living creatures are bad results. ^an10-173-2-5

And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of not killing living creatures are good results. ^an10-173-2-6

Stealing is a bad principle. ^an10-173-3-1

Not stealing is a good principle. … ^an10-173-3-2

Sexual misconduct is a bad principle. ^an10-173-3-3

Avoiding sexual misconduct is a good principle. … ^an10-173-3-4

Lying is a bad principle. ^an10-173-3-5

Not lying is a good principle. … ^an10-173-3-6

Backbiting is a bad principle. ^an10-173-3-7

Avoiding backbiting is a good principle. … ^an10-173-3-8

Harsh speech is a bad principle. ^an10-173-3-9

Avoiding harsh speech is a good principle. … ^an10-173-3-10

Talking nonsense is a bad principle. ^an10-173-3-11

Avoiding talking nonsense is a good principle. … ^an10-173-3-12

Covetousness is a bad principle. ^an10-173-3-13

Contentment is a good principle. … ^an10-173-3-14

Ill will is a bad principle. ^an10-173-3-15

Good will is a good principle. … ^an10-173-3-16

Wrong view is a bad principle. ^an10-173-4-1

Right view is a good principle. ^an10-173-4-2

And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by wrong view are bad results. ^an10-173-4-3

And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of right view are good results. ^an10-173-4-4

‘You should know bad principles and good principles. ^an10-173-5-1

And you should know bad results and good results. ^an10-173-5-2

Knowing these things, your practice should follow the good principles with good results.’ ^an10-173-5-3

That’s what I said, and this is why I said it.” ^an10-173-5-4

### Sources of Deeds (Numbered Discourses 10.174) ^an10-174-0

“Mendicants, I say that killing living creatures is threefold: ^an10-174-1-1

caused by greed, hate, or delusion. ^an10-174-1-2

I say that stealing is threefold: ^an10-174-2-1

caused by greed, hate, or delusion. ^an10-174-2-2

I say that sexual misconduct is threefold: ^an10-174-3-1

caused by greed, hate, or delusion. ^an10-174-3-2

I say that lying is threefold: ^an10-174-4-1

caused by greed, hate, or delusion. ^an10-174-4-2

I say that backbiting is threefold: ^an10-174-5-1

caused by greed, hate, or delusion. ^an10-174-5-2

I say that harsh speech is threefold: ^an10-174-6-1

caused by greed, hate, or delusion. ^an10-174-6-2

I say that talking nonsense is threefold: ^an10-174-7-1

caused by greed, hate, or delusion. ^an10-174-7-2

I say that covetousness is threefold: ^an10-174-8-1

caused by greed, hate, or delusion. ^an10-174-8-2

I say that ill will is threefold: ^an10-174-9-1

caused by greed, hate, or delusion. ^an10-174-9-2

I say that wrong view is threefold: ^an10-174-10-1

caused by greed, hate, or delusion. ^an10-174-10-2

And so greed, hate, and delusion are sources and origins for deeds. ^an10-174-10-3

With the ending of greed, hate, and delusion, the sources of deeds are ended.” ^an10-174-10-4

### The Bypass (Numbered Discourses 10.175) ^an10-175-0

“Mendicants, this teaching provides a bypass, it doesn’t lack a bypass. ^an10-175-1-1

And how does this teaching provide a bypass, not lacking a bypass? ^an10-175-1-2

Not killing living creatures bypasses killing living creatures. ^an10-175-1-3

Not stealing bypasses stealing. ^an10-175-1-4

Avoiding sexual misconduct bypasses sexual misconduct. ^an10-175-1-5

Not lying bypasses lying. ^an10-175-1-6

Avoiding backbiting bypasses backbiting. ^an10-175-1-7

Avoiding harsh speech bypasses harsh speech. ^an10-175-1-8

Avoiding talking nonsense bypasses talking nonsense. ^an10-175-1-9

Contentment bypasses covetousness. ^an10-175-1-10

Good will bypasses ill will. ^an10-175-1-11

Right view bypasses wrong view. ^an10-175-1-12

That’s how this teaching provides a bypass, it doesn’t lack a bypass.” ^an10-175-1-13

### With Cunda (Numbered Discourses 10.176) ^an10-176-0

So I have heard. ^an10-176-1-1

At one time the Buddha was staying near Pāvā in Cunda the smith’s mango grove. ^an10-176-1-2

Then Cunda the smith went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him, ^an10-176-1-3

“Cunda, whose purity do you believe in?” ^an10-176-1-4

“Sir, I believe in the purity advocated by the western brahmins garlanded with aquatic flowers who carry pitchers, serve the sacred flame, and immerse themselves in water.” ^an10-176-1-5

“But Cunda, what kind of purity do these western brahmins advocate?” ^an10-176-2-1

“The western brahmins encourage their disciple like this: ^an10-176-2-2

‘Please, my man, rising early you should stroke the earth from your bed. ^an10-176-2-3

If you don’t stroke the earth, stroke fresh cow dung. ^an10-176-2-4

If you don’t stroke fresh cow dung, stroke green grass. ^an10-176-2-5

If you don’t stroke green grass, serve the sacred flame. ^an10-176-2-6

If you don’t serve the sacred flame, revere the sun with cupped palms. ^an10-176-2-7

If you don’t revere the sun with cupped palms, immerse yourself in water three times, including at dusk.’ ^an10-176-2-8

The western brahmins advocate this kind of purity.” ^an10-176-2-9

“The purity advocated by the western brahmins is quite different from that in the training of the Noble One.” ^an10-176-3-1

“But what, sir, is purity in the training of the Noble One? ^an10-176-3-2

Sir, please teach me this.” ^an10-176-3-3

“Well then, brahmin, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.” ^an10-176-4-1

“Yes, sir,” Cunda replied. ^an10-176-4-2

The Buddha said this: ^an10-176-4-3

“Cunda, impurity is threefold by way of body, ^an10-176-5-1

fourfold by way of speech, ^an10-176-5-2

and threefold by way of mind. ^an10-176-5-3

And how is impurity threefold by way of body? ^an10-176-6-1

It’s when a certain person kills living creatures. They’re violent, bloody-handed, a hardened killer, merciless to living beings. ^an10-176-6-2

They steal. With the intention to commit theft, they take the wealth or belongings of others from village or wilderness. ^an10-176-7-1

They commit sexual misconduct. They have sexual relations with women who have their mother, father, both mother and father, brother, sister, relatives, or clan as guardian. They have sexual relations with a woman who is protected on principle, or who has a husband, or whose violation is punishable by law, or even one who has been garlanded as a token of betrothal. ^an10-176-8-1

This is the threefold impurity by way of body. ^an10-176-9-1

And how is impurity fourfold by way of speech? ^an10-176-10-1

It’s when a certain person lies. They’re summoned to a council, an assembly, a family meeting, a guild, or to the royal court, and asked to bear witness: ‘Please, my man, say what you know.’ Not knowing, they say ‘I know.’ Knowing, they say ‘I don’t know.’ Not seeing, they say ‘I see.’ And seeing, they say ‘I don’t see.’ So they deliberately lie for the sake of themselves or another, or for some trivial worldly reason. ^an10-176-10-2

They backbite. They repeat in one place what they heard in another so as to divide people against each other. And so they divide those who are harmonious, supporting division, delighting in division, loving division, speaking words that promote division. ^an10-176-11-1

They speak harshly. They use the kinds of words that are cruel, nasty, hurtful, offensive, bordering on anger, not leading to immersion. ^an10-176-12-1

They talk nonsense. Their speech is untimely, and is neither factual nor beneficial. It has nothing to do with the teaching or the training. Their words have no value, and are untimely, unreasonable, rambling, and pointless. ^an10-176-13-1

This is the fourfold impurity by way of speech. ^an10-176-13-2

And how is impurity threefold by way of mind? ^an10-176-14-1

It’s when a certain person is covetous. They covet the wealth and belongings of others: ‘Oh, if only their belongings were mine!’ ^an10-176-14-2

They have ill will and malicious intentions: ‘May these sentient beings be killed, slaughtered, slain, destroyed, or annihilated!’ ^an10-176-15-1

They have wrong view. Their perspective is distorted: ^an10-176-16-1

‘There’s no meaning in giving, sacrifice, or offerings. There’s no fruit or result of good and bad deeds. There’s no afterlife. There’s no such thing as mother and father, or beings that are reborn spontaneously. And there’s no ascetic or brahmin who is rightly comported and rightly practiced, and who describes the afterlife after realizing it with their own insight.’ ^an10-176-16-2

This is the threefold impurity by way of mind. ^an10-176-16-3

These are the ten ways of doing unskillful deeds. ^an10-176-17-1

When you have these ten ways of doing unskillful deeds, then if you rise early, whether or not you stroke the earth from your bed, you’re still impure. ^an10-176-17-2

Whether or not you stroke fresh cow dung, you’re still impure. ^an10-176-18-1

Whether or not you stroke green grass, you’re still impure. ^an10-176-19-1

Whether or not you serve the sacred flame, you’re still impure. ^an10-176-20-1

Whether or not you revere the sun with cupped palms, you’re still impure. ^an10-176-21-1

Whether or not you immerse yourself in water three times, you’re still impure. ^an10-176-22-1

Why is that? ^an10-176-22-2

These ten ways of doing unskillful deeds are impure and make things impure. ^an10-176-22-3

It’s because of those who do these ten kinds of unskillful deeds that hell, the animal realm, the ghost realm, or any other bad places are found. ^an10-176-23-1

Cunda, purity is threefold by way of body, ^an10-176-24-1

fourfold by way of speech, ^an10-176-24-2

and threefold by way of mind. ^an10-176-24-3

And how is purity threefold by way of body? ^an10-176-25-1

It’s when a certain person gives up killing living creatures. They renounce the rod and the sword. They’re scrupulous and kind, living full of sympathy for all living beings. ^an10-176-25-2

They give up stealing. They don’t, with the intention to commit theft, take the wealth or belongings of others from village or wilderness. ^an10-176-26-1

They give up sexual misconduct. They don’t have sexual relations with women who have their mother, father, both mother and father, brother, sister, relatives, or clan as guardian. They don’t have sexual relations with a woman who is protected on principle, or who has a husband, or whose violation is punishable by law, or even one who has been garlanded as a token of betrothal. ^an10-176-27-1

This is the threefold purity by way of body. ^an10-176-28-1

And how is purity fourfold by way of speech? ^an10-176-29-1

It’s when a certain person gives up lying. They’re summoned to a council, an assembly, a family meeting, a guild, or to the royal court, and asked to bear witness: ‘Please, my man, say what you know.’ Not knowing, they say ‘I don’t know.’ Knowing, they say ‘I know.’ Not seeing, they say ‘I don’t see.’ And seeing, they say ‘I see.’ So they don’t deliberately lie for the sake of themselves or another, or for some trivial worldly reason. ^an10-176-29-2

They give up backbiting. They don’t repeat in one place what they heard in another so as to divide people against each other. Instead, they reconcile those who are divided and support those who are united, delighting in harmony, loving harmony, speaking words that promote harmony. ^an10-176-30-1

They give up harsh speech. They speak in a way that’s mellow, pleasing to the ear, endearing, going to the heart, polite, likable and agreeable to the people. ^an10-176-31-1

They give up talking nonsense. Their words are timely, true, and meaningful, in line with the teaching and training. ^an10-176-32-1

They say things at the right time which are valuable, reasonable, succinct, and beneficial. ^an10-176-32-2

This is the fourfold purity by way of speech. ^an10-176-33-1

And how is purity threefold by way of mind? ^an10-176-34-1

It’s when a certain person is content. They don’t covet the wealth and belongings of others: ‘Oh, if only their belongings were mine!’ ^an10-176-34-2

They have a kind heart and loving intentions: ‘May these sentient beings live free of enmity and ill will, untroubled and happy!’ ^an10-176-35-1

They have right view, an undistorted perspective: ^an10-176-36-1

‘There is meaning in giving, sacrifice, and offerings. There are fruits and results of good and bad deeds. There is an afterlife. There are such things as mother and father, and beings that are reborn spontaneously. And there are ascetics and brahmins who are rightly comported and rightly practiced, and who describe the afterlife after realizing it with their own insight.’ ^an10-176-36-2

This is the threefold purity by way of mind. ^an10-176-37-1

These are the ten ways of doing skillful deeds. ^an10-176-38-1

When you have these ten ways of doing skillful deeds, then if you rise early, whether or not you stroke the earth from your bed, you’re still pure. ^an10-176-38-2

Whether or not you stroke fresh cow dung, you’re still pure. ^an10-176-39-1

Whether or not you stroke green grass, you’re still pure. ^an10-176-40-1

Whether or not you serve the sacred flame, you’re still pure. ^an10-176-41-1

Whether or not you revere the sun with cupped palms, you’re still pure. ^an10-176-42-1

Whether or not you immerse yourself in water three times, you’re still pure. ^an10-176-43-1

Why is that? ^an10-176-43-2

These ten ways of doing skillful deeds are pure and make things pure. ^an10-176-43-3

It’s because of those who do these ten kinds of skillful deeds that gods, humans, or any other good places are found.” ^an10-176-44-1

When he said this, Cunda the smith said to the Buddha, ^an10-176-45-1

“Excellent, sir! Excellent! … ^an10-176-45-2

From this day forth, may the Buddha remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.” ^an10-176-45-3

### With Jānussoṇi (Numbered Discourses 10.177) ^an10-177-0

Then the brahmin Jānussoṇi went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him. ^an10-177-1-1

When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha, ^an10-177-1-2

“We who are known as brahmins ^an10-177-2-1

give gifts and perform memorial rites for the dead: ^an10-177-2-2

‘May this gift aid my departed relatives and kin. May they partake of this gift.’ ^an10-177-2-3

But does this gift really aid departed relatives and kin? ^an10-177-2-4

Do they actually partake of it?” ^an10-177-2-5

“It aids them if the conditions are right, brahmin, but not if the conditions are wrong.” ^an10-177-2-6

“Then, worthy Gotama, what are the right and wrong conditions?” ^an10-177-3-1

“Brahmin, take someone who kills living creatures, steals, and commits sexual misconduct. They use speech that’s false, backbiting, harsh, or nonsensical. And they’re covetous, malicious, with wrong view. ^an10-177-3-2

When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in hell. ^an10-177-3-3

There they survive feeding on the food of the hell beings. ^an10-177-3-4

The conditions there are wrong, so the gift does not aid the one who lives there. ^an10-177-3-5

Take someone else who kills living creatures … and has wrong view. ^an10-177-4-1

When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the animal realm. ^an10-177-4-2

There they survive feeding on the food of the beings in the animal realm. ^an10-177-4-3

The conditions there too are wrong, so the gift does not aid the one who lives there. ^an10-177-4-4

Take someone else who doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, or use speech that’s false, backbiting, harsh, or nonsensical. They’re contented, kind-hearted, and have right view. ^an10-177-5-1

When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the human realm. ^an10-177-5-2

There they survive feeding on human food. ^an10-177-5-3

The conditions there too are wrong, so the gift does not aid the one who lives there. ^an10-177-5-4

Take someone else who doesn’t kill living creatures … and has right view. ^an10-177-6-1

When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the company of the gods. ^an10-177-6-2

There they survive feeding on the food of the gods. ^an10-177-6-3

The conditions there too are wrong, so the gift does not aid the one who lives there. ^an10-177-6-4

Take someone else who kills living creatures … and has wrong view. ^an10-177-7-1

When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the ghost realm. ^an10-177-7-2

There they survive feeding on the food of the beings in the ghost realm. Or else they survive feeding on what friends and colleagues, relatives and kin provide them with from here. ^an10-177-7-3

The conditions there are right, so the gift aids the one who lives there.” ^an10-177-7-4

“But worthy Gotama, who partakes of that gift if the departed relative is not reborn in that place?” ^an10-177-8-1

“Other departed relatives reborn there will partake of that gift.” ^an10-177-8-2

“But who partakes of the gift when neither that relative nor other relatives have been reborn in that place?” ^an10-177-9-1

“It’s impossible, brahmin, it cannot happen that that place is vacant of departed relatives in all this long time. ^an10-177-9-2

It’s never fruitless for the donor.” ^an10-177-9-3

“Does the worthy Gotama propose this even when the conditions are wrong?” ^an10-177-10-1

“I propose this even when the conditions are wrong. ^an10-177-10-2

Take someone who kills living creatures, steals, and commits sexual misconduct. They use speech that’s false, backbiting, harsh, or nonsensical. And they’re covetous, malicious, with wrong view. ^an10-177-10-3

They give to ascetics or brahmins such things as food, drink, clothing, vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; and bed, house, and lighting. ^an10-177-10-4

When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the company of elephants. ^an10-177-10-5

There they get to have food and drink, garlands and various adornments. ^an10-177-10-6

Since in this life they killed living creatures … and had wrong view, they were reborn in the company of elephants. ^an10-177-11-1

Since they gave to ascetics or brahmins … they get to have food and drink, garlands and various adornments. ^an10-177-11-2

Take someone else who kills living creatures … and has wrong view. ^an10-177-12-1

They give to ascetics or brahmins … ^an10-177-12-2

When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the company of horses. … ^an10-177-12-3

cattle … ^an10-177-12-4

dogs. ^an10-177-12-5

There they get to have food and drink, garlands and various adornments. ^an10-177-12-6

Since in this life they killed living creatures … and had wrong view, they were reborn in the company of dogs. ^an10-177-13-1

Since they gave to ascetics or brahmins … they get to have food and drink, garlands and various adornments. ^an10-177-13-2

Take someone else who doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, or commit sexual misconduct. They don’t use speech that’s false, backbiting, harsh, or nonsensical. And they’re contented, kind-hearted, with right view. ^an10-177-14-1

They give to ascetics or brahmins … ^an10-177-14-2

When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the human realm. ^an10-177-14-3

There they get to have the five kinds of human sensual stimulation. ^an10-177-14-4

Since in this life they didn’t kill living creatures … and had right view, they were reborn in the company of humans. ^an10-177-15-1

Since they gave to ascetics or brahmins … they get to have the five kinds of human sensual stimulation. ^an10-177-15-2

Take someone else who doesn’t kill living creatures … and has right view. ^an10-177-16-1

They give to ascetics or brahmins … ^an10-177-16-2

When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the company of the gods. ^an10-177-16-3

There they get to have the five kinds of heavenly sensual stimulation. ^an10-177-16-4

Since in this life they didn’t kill living creatures … and had right view, they were reborn in the company of the gods. ^an10-177-17-1

Since they gave to ascetics or brahmins … they get to have the five kinds of heavenly sensual stimulation. ^an10-177-17-2

It’s never fruitless for the donor.” ^an10-177-17-3

“It’s incredible, worthy Gotama, it’s amazing, ^an10-177-18-1

This is quite enough to justify giving gifts and performing memorial rites for the dead, since it’s never fruitless for the donor.” ^an10-177-18-2

“That’s so true, brahmin. It’s never fruitless for the donor.” ^an10-177-18-3

“Excellent, worthy Gotama! Excellent! … ^an10-177-19-1

From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.” ^an10-177-19-2

### Good (Numbered Discourses 10.178) ^an10-178-0

“Mendicants, I will teach you what is good and what is not good. ^an10-178-1-1

Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.” ^an10-178-1-2

“Yes, sir,” they replied. ^an10-178-1-3

The Buddha said this: ^an10-178-1-4

“And what, mendicants, is not good? ^an10-178-2-1

Killing living creatures, stealing, and sexual misconduct; speech that’s false, backbiting, harsh, or nonsensical; covetousness, ill will, and wrong view. ^an10-178-2-2

This is called what is not good. ^an10-178-2-3

And what is good? ^an10-178-3-1

Avoiding killing living creatures, stealing, and sexual misconduct; avoiding speech that’s false, backbiting, harsh, or nonsensical; contentment, good will, and right view. ^an10-178-3-2

This is called what is good.” ^an10-178-3-3

### The Teaching of the Noble Ones (Numbered Discourses 10.179) ^an10-179-0

“Mendicants, I will teach you the teaching of the noble ones, and what is not the teaching of the noble ones. ^an10-179-1-1

Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak. … ^an10-179-1-2

And what is not the teaching of the noble ones? ^an10-179-1-3

Killing living creatures … ^an10-179-1-4

wrong view. ^an10-179-1-5

This is called what is not the teaching of the noble ones. ^an10-179-1-6

And what is the teaching of the noble ones? ^an10-179-2-1

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-179-2-2

right view. ^an10-179-2-3

This is called the teaching of the noble ones.” ^an10-179-2-4

### Skillful (Numbered Discourses 10.180) ^an10-180-0

“I will teach you the skillful and the unskillful … ^an10-180-1-1

And what is the unskillful? ^an10-180-1-3

Killing living creatures … ^an10-180-1-4

wrong view. ^an10-180-1-5

This is called the unskillful. ^an10-180-1-6

And what is the skillful? ^an10-180-2-1

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-180-2-2

right view. ^an10-180-2-3

This is called the skillful.” ^an10-180-2-4

### Beneficial (Numbered Discourses 10.181) ^an10-181-0

“I will teach you the beneficial and the harmful. … ^an10-181-1-1

And what is the harmful? ^an10-181-1-3

Killing living creatures … ^an10-181-1-4

wrong view. ^an10-181-1-5

This is called the harmful. ^an10-181-1-6

And what is the beneficial? ^an10-181-2-1

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-181-2-2

right view. ^an10-181-2-3

This is called the beneficial.” ^an10-181-2-4

### The Teaching (Numbered Discourses 10.182) ^an10-182-0

“I will teach you what is the teaching and what is not the teaching. … ^an10-182-1-1

And what is not the teaching? ^an10-182-1-3

Killing living creatures … ^an10-182-1-4

wrong view. ^an10-182-1-5

This is called what is not the teaching. ^an10-182-1-6

And what is the teaching? ^an10-182-2-1

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-182-2-2

right view. ^an10-182-2-3

This is called the teaching.” ^an10-182-2-4

### Defiled (Numbered Discourses 10.183) ^an10-183-0

“I will teach you the defiled principle and the undefiled. … ^an10-183-1-1

And what is the defiled principle? ^an10-183-1-3

Killing living creatures … ^an10-183-1-4

wrong view. ^an10-183-1-5

This is called the defiled principle. ^an10-183-1-6

And what is the undefiled principle? ^an10-183-2-1

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-183-2-2

right view. ^an10-183-2-3

This is called the undefiled principle.” ^an10-183-2-4

### Blameworthy (Numbered Discourses 10.184) ^an10-184-0

“I will teach you the blameworthy principle and the blameless. … ^an10-184-1-1

And what is the blameworthy principle? ^an10-184-1-3

Killing living creatures … ^an10-184-1-4

wrong view. ^an10-184-1-5

This is called the blameworthy principle. ^an10-184-1-6

And what is the blameless principle? ^an10-184-2-1

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-184-2-2

right view. ^an10-184-2-3

This is called the blameless principle.” ^an10-184-2-4

### Mortifying (Numbered Discourses 10.185) ^an10-185-0

“I will teach you the mortifying principle and the unmortifying. … ^an10-185-1-1

And what is the mortifying principle? ^an10-185-1-3

Killing living creatures … ^an10-185-1-4

wrong view. ^an10-185-1-5

This is called the mortifying principle. ^an10-185-1-6

And what is the unmortifying principle? ^an10-185-2-1

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-185-2-2

right view. ^an10-185-2-3

This is called the unmortifying principle.” ^an10-185-2-4

### Leading to Accumulation (Numbered Discourses 10.186) ^an10-186-0

“I will teach you the principle that leads to accumulation and that which leads to dispersal. … ^an10-186-1-1

And what is the principle that leads to accumulation? ^an10-186-1-3

Killing living creatures … ^an10-186-1-4

wrong view. ^an10-186-1-5

This is called the principle that leads to accumulation. ^an10-186-1-6

And what is the principle that leads to dispersal? ^an10-186-2-1

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-186-2-2

right view. ^an10-186-2-3

This is called the principle that leads to dispersal.” ^an10-186-2-4

### With Suffering as Outcome (Numbered Discourses 10.187) ^an10-187-0

“I will teach you the principle that has suffering as outcome, and that which has happiness as outcome. … ^an10-187-1-1

And what is the principle whose outcome is suffering? ^an10-187-1-3

Killing living creatures … ^an10-187-1-4

wrong view. ^an10-187-1-5

This is the principle whose outcome is suffering. ^an10-187-1-6

And what is the principle whose outcome is happiness? ^an10-187-2-1

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-187-2-2

right view. ^an10-187-2-3

This is the principle whose outcome is happiness.” ^an10-187-2-4

### Result (Numbered Discourses 10.188) ^an10-188-0

“I will teach you the principle that results in suffering and that which results in happiness. … ^an10-188-1-1

And what is the principle that results in suffering? ^an10-188-1-3

Killing living creatures … ^an10-188-1-4

wrong view. ^an10-188-1-5

This is called the principle that results in suffering. ^an10-188-1-6

And what is the principle that results in happiness? ^an10-188-2-1

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-188-2-2

right view. ^an10-188-2-3

This is called the principle that results in happiness.” ^an10-188-2-4

### The Noble Path (Numbered Discourses 10.189) ^an10-189-0

“I will teach you the noble path and the ignoble path. … ^an10-189-1-1

And what is the ignoble path? ^an10-189-1-3

Killing living creatures … ^an10-189-1-4

wrong view. ^an10-189-1-5

This is called the ignoble path. ^an10-189-1-6

And what is the noble path? ^an10-189-2-1

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-189-2-2

right view. ^an10-189-2-3

This is called the noble path.” ^an10-189-2-4

### The Dark Path (Numbered Discourses 10.190) ^an10-190-0

“I will teach you the dark path and the bright path. … ^an10-190-1-1

And what is the dark path? ^an10-190-1-3

Killing living creatures … ^an10-190-1-4

wrong view. ^an10-190-1-5

This is called the dark path. ^an10-190-1-6

And what is the bright path? ^an10-190-2-1

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-190-2-2

right view. ^an10-190-2-3

This is called the bright path.” ^an10-190-2-4

wrong view. ^an10-191-1-5

This is called what is not the true teaching. ^an10-191-1-6

Not killing living creatures … ^an10-191-2-2

right view. ^an10-191-2-3

This is called the true teaching.” ^an10-191-2-4

wrong view. ^an10-192-1-5

right view. ^an10-192-2-3

### Should Not Associate, Etc. (Numbered Discourses 10.199–210) ^an10-199-210-0

“Mendicants, you should not associate with an individual who has ten qualities. ^an10-199-210-1-1

What ten? ^an10-199-210-1-2

They kill living creatures, steal, and commit sexual misconduct. They use speech that’s false, backbiting, harsh, or nonsensical. And they’re covetous, malicious, with wrong view. ^an10-199-210-1-3

You should not associate with an individual who has these ten qualities. ^an10-199-210-1-4

You should associate with an individual who has ten qualities. ^an10-199-210-2-1

What ten? ^an10-199-210-2-2

They don’t kill living creatures, steal, or commit sexual misconduct. They don’t use speech that’s false, backbiting, harsh, or nonsensical. They’re contented, kind-hearted, with right view. ^an10-199-210-2-3

You should associate with an individual who has these ten qualities.” ^an10-199-210-2-4

“Mendicants, you should not frequent an individual who has ten qualities … ^an10-199-210-3-1

You should frequent …” ^an10-199-210-3-2

“You should not pay homage … ^an10-199-210-3-3

You should pay homage …” ^an10-199-210-3-4

“You should not venerate … ^an10-199-210-3-5

You should venerate …” ^an10-199-210-3-6

“You should not praise … ^an10-199-210-3-7

You should praise …” ^an10-199-210-3-8

“You should not revere … ^an10-199-210-3-9

You should revere …” ^an10-199-210-3-10

“You should not defer to … ^an10-199-210-3-11

You should defer to …” ^an10-199-210-3-12

“Someone is not a success … ^an10-199-210-3-13

Someone is a success …” ^an10-199-210-3-14

“Someone is not pure … ^an10-199-210-3-15

Someone is pure …” ^an10-199-210-3-16

“Someone does not win over conceit … ^an10-199-210-3-17

Someone wins over conceit …” ^an10-199-210-3-18

“Someone does not grow in wisdom … ^an10-199-210-3-19

Someone grows in wisdom …” ^an10-199-210-3-20

“An individual who possesses these ten qualities brims with much wickedness. … ^an10-199-210-4-1

An individual who possesses these ten qualities brims with much merit. ^an10-199-210-4-2

What ten? ^an10-199-210-4-3

They don’t kill living creatures, steal, or commit sexual misconduct. They don’t use speech that’s false, backbiting, harsh, or nonsensical. They’re contented, kind-hearted, with right view. ^an10-199-210-4-4

An individual who has these ten qualities creates much merit.” ^an10-199-210-4-5

### Heaven and Hell (1st) (Numbered Discourses 10.211) ^an10-211-0

“Someone with ten qualities is placed in hell as if delivered there. ^an10-211-1-1

What ten? ^an10-211-1-2

It’s when a certain person kills living creatures. They’re violent, bloody-handed, a hardened killer, merciless to living beings. ^an10-211-1-3

They steal. With the intention to commit theft, they take the wealth or belongings of others from village or wilderness. ^an10-211-2-1

They commit sexual misconduct. They have sexual relations with women who have their mother, father, both mother and father, brother, sister, relatives, or clan as guardian. They have sexual relations with a woman who is protected on principle, or who has a husband, or whose violation is punishable by law, or even one who has been garlanded as a token of betrothal. ^an10-211-3-1

They lie. They’re summoned to a council, an assembly, a family meeting, a guild, or to the royal court, and asked to bear witness: ‘Please, my man, say what you know.’ Not knowing, they say ‘I know.’ Knowing, they say ‘I don’t know.’ Not seeing, they say ‘I see.’ And seeing, they say ‘I don’t see.’ So they deliberately lie for the sake of themselves or another, or for some trivial worldly reason. ^an10-211-4-1

They backbite. They repeat in one place what they heard in another so as to divide people against each other. And so they divide those who are harmonious, supporting division, delighting in division, loving division, speaking words that promote division. ^an10-211-5-1

They speak harshly. They use the kinds of words that are cruel, nasty, hurtful, offensive, bordering on anger, not leading to immersion. ^an10-211-6-1

They talk nonsense. Their speech is untimely, and is neither factual nor beneficial. It has nothing to do with the teaching or the training. Their words have no value, and are untimely, unreasonable, rambling, and pointless. ^an10-211-7-1

They’re covetous. They covet the wealth and belongings of others: ‘Oh, if only their belongings were mine!’ ^an10-211-8-1

They have ill will and malicious intentions: ‘May these sentient beings be killed, slaughtered, slain, destroyed, or annihilated!’ ^an10-211-9-1

They have wrong view. Their perspective is distorted: ^an10-211-10-1

‘There’s no meaning in giving, sacrifice, or offerings. There’s no fruit or result of good and bad deeds. There’s no afterlife. There’s no such thing as mother and father, or beings that are reborn spontaneously. And there’s no ascetic or brahmin who is rightly comported and rightly practiced, and who describes the afterlife after realizing it with their own insight.’ ^an10-211-11-1

Someone with these ten qualities is placed in hell as if delivered there. ^an10-211-11-2

Someone with ten qualities is placed in heaven as if delivered there. ^an10-211-12-1

What ten? ^an10-211-12-2

It’s when a certain person gives up killing living creatures. They renounce the rod and the sword. They’re scrupulous and kind, living full of sympathy for all living beings. ^an10-211-12-3

They give up stealing. They don’t, with the intention to commit theft, take the wealth or belongings of others from village or wilderness. ^an10-211-13-1

They give up sexual misconduct. They don’t have sexual relations with women who have their mother, father, both mother and father, brother, sister, relatives, or clan as guardian. They don’t have sexual relations with a woman who is protected on principle, or who has a husband, or whose violation is punishable by law, or even one who has been garlanded as a token of betrothal. ^an10-211-14-1

They give up lying. They’re summoned to a council, an assembly, a family meeting, a guild, or to the royal court, and asked to bear witness: ‘Please, my man, say what you know.’ Not knowing, they say ‘I don’t know.’ Knowing, they say ‘I know.’ Not seeing, they say ‘I don’t see.’ And seeing, they say ‘I see.’ So they don’t deliberately lie for the sake of themselves or another, or for some trivial worldly reason. ^an10-211-15-1

They give up backbiting. They don’t repeat in one place what they heard in another so as to divide people against each other. Instead, they reconcile those who are divided and support those who are united, delighting in harmony, loving harmony, speaking words that promote harmony. ^an10-211-16-1

They give up harsh speech. They speak in a way that’s mellow, pleasing to the ear, endearing, going to the heart, polite, likable and agreeable to the people. ^an10-211-17-1

They give up talking nonsense. Their words are timely, true, and meaningful, in line with the teaching and training. They say things at the right time which are valuable, reasonable, succinct, and beneficial. ^an10-211-18-1

They’re content. They don’t covet the wealth and belongings of others: ‘Oh, if only their belongings were mine!’ ^an10-211-19-1

They have a kind heart and loving intentions: ‘May these sentient beings live free of enmity and ill will, untroubled and happy!’ ^an10-211-20-1

They have right view, an undistorted perspective: ^an10-211-21-1

‘There is meaning in giving, sacrifice, and offerings. There are fruits and results of good and bad deeds. There is an afterlife. There are such things as mother and father, and beings that are reborn spontaneously. And there are ascetics and brahmins who are rightly comported and rightly practiced, and who describe the afterlife after realizing it with their own insight.’ ^an10-211-21-2

Someone with these ten qualities is placed in heaven as if delivered there.” ^an10-211-22-1

### Assured (Numbered Discourses 10.215) ^an10-215-0

What ten? ^an10-215-1-2

She kills living creatures. … ^an10-215-1-3

She has wrong view. … ^an10-215-1-12

A laywoman living at home with these ten qualities is not self-assured. ^an10-215-1-13

She doesn’t kill living creatures. … ^an10-215-2-3

She has right view. … ^an10-215-2-12

### Creepy Creatures (Numbered Discourses 10.216) ^an10-216-0

“Mendicants, I will teach you an exposition of the teaching on creepy creatures. ^an10-216-1-1

Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.” ^an10-216-1-2

“Yes, sir,” they replied. ^an10-216-1-3

The Buddha said this: ^an10-216-1-4

“What is the exposition of the teaching on creepy creatures? ^an10-216-2-1

Sentient beings are the owners of their deeds and heir to their deeds. Deeds are their womb, their relative, and their refuge. ^an10-216-2-2

They shall be the heir of whatever deeds they do, whether good or bad. ^an10-216-2-3

Take a certain someone who kills living creatures. They’re violent, bloody-handed, a hardened killer, merciless to living beings. ^an10-216-3-1

They’re creepy in body, speech, and mind. ^an10-216-3-2

Doing crooked deeds by way of body, speech, and mind, their destiny and rebirth are crooked. ^an10-216-3-3

Someone whose destiny and rebirth is crooked is reborn in one of two places, I say: ^an10-216-4-1

in an exclusively painful hell, or among the species of creepy animals. ^an10-216-4-2

And what are the species of creepy animals? ^an10-216-4-3

Snakes, scorpions, centipedes, mongooses, cats, mice, owls, or whatever other species of animal that creep away when they see humans. ^an10-216-4-4

This is how a being is born from a being. ^an10-216-4-5

For your deeds determine your rebirth, ^an10-216-4-6

and when you’re reborn contacts strike you. ^an10-216-4-7

This is why I say that sentient beings are heirs to their deeds. ^an10-216-4-8

Take someone else who steals … ^an10-216-5-1

commits sexual misconduct … ^an10-216-5-2

lies … ^an10-216-5-3

backbites … ^an10-216-5-4

speaks harshly … ^an10-216-5-5

indulges in talking nonsense … ^an10-216-5-6

is covetous … ^an10-216-5-7

has cruel intentions … ^an10-216-5-8

has wrong view … ^an10-216-5-9

They’re creepy in body, speech, and mind. ^an10-216-5-12

Doing crooked deeds by way of body, speech, and mind, their destiny and rebirth are crooked. ^an10-216-5-13

Someone whose destiny and rebirth is crooked is reborn in one of two places, I say: ^an10-216-6-1

in an exclusively painful hell, or among the species of creepy animals. ^an10-216-6-2

And what are the species of creepy animals? ^an10-216-6-3

Snakes, scorpions, centipedes, mongooses, cats, mice, owls, or whatever other species of animal that creep away when they see humans. ^an10-216-6-4

This is how a being is born from a being. ^an10-216-6-5

For your deeds determine your rebirth, ^an10-216-6-6

and when you’re reborn contacts strike you. ^an10-216-6-7

This is why I say that sentient beings are heirs to their deeds. ^an10-216-6-8

Sentient beings are the owners of their deeds and heir to their deeds. Deeds are their womb, their relative, and their refuge. ^an10-216-6-9

They shall be the heir of whatever deeds they do, whether good or bad. ^an10-216-6-10

Take a certain someone who gives up killing living creatures. They renounce the rod and the sword. They’re scrupulous and kind, living full of sympathy for all living beings. ^an10-216-7-1

They’re not creepy in body, speech, and mind. ^an10-216-7-2

Doing virtuous deeds by way of body, speech, and mind, their destiny and rebirth is virtuous. ^an10-216-7-3

Someone whose destiny and rebirth is virtuous is reborn in one of two places, I say: ^an10-216-8-1

in a heaven of perfect happiness, or in an eminent well-to-do family of aristocrats, brahmins, or householders—rich, affluent, and wealthy, with lots of gold and silver, lots of property and assets, and lots of money and grain. ^an10-216-8-2

This is how a being is born from a being. ^an10-216-8-3

For your deeds determine your rebirth, ^an10-216-8-4

and when you’re reborn contacts strike you. ^an10-216-8-5

This is why I say that sentient beings are heirs to their deeds. ^an10-216-8-6

Take someone else who gives up stealing … ^an10-216-9-1

sexual misconduct … ^an10-216-9-2

lying … ^an10-216-9-3

backbiting … ^an10-216-9-4

harsh speech … ^an10-216-9-5

talking nonsense … ^an10-216-9-6

They’re content … ^an10-216-9-7

kind hearted … ^an10-216-9-8

they have right view … ^an10-216-9-9

They’re not creepy in body, speech, and mind. ^an10-216-9-12

Doing virtuous deeds by way of body, speech, and mind, their destiny and rebirth is virtuous. ^an10-216-9-13

Someone whose destiny and rebirth is virtuous is reborn in one of two places, I say: ^an10-216-10-1

in a heaven of perfect happiness, or in an eminent well-to-do family of aristocrats, brahmins, or householders—rich, affluent, and wealthy, with lots of gold and silver, lots of property and assets, and lots of money and grain. ^an10-216-10-2

This is how a being is born from a being. ^an10-216-10-3

For your deeds determine your rebirth, ^an10-216-10-4

and when you’re reborn contacts strike you. ^an10-216-10-5

This is why I say that sentient beings are heirs to their deeds. ^an10-216-10-6

Sentient beings are the owners of their deeds and heir to their deeds. Deeds are their womb, their relative, and their refuge. ^an10-216-11-1

They shall be the heir of whatever deeds they do, whether good or bad. ^an10-216-11-2

This is the exposition of the teaching on creepy creatures.” ^an10-216-11-3

### Intentional (1st) (Numbered Discourses 10.217) ^an10-217-0

“Mendicants, I don’t say that intentional deeds that have been performed and accumulated are eliminated without being experienced. ^an10-217-1-1

And that may be in this very life, or in the next life, or in some subsequent period. ^an10-217-1-2

But in any case, there is no making an end of suffering without experiencing intentional deeds that have been performed and accumulated, I say. ^an10-217-1-3

Now, there are three kinds of corruption and failure of bodily action that have unskillful intention, with suffering as their outcome and result. ^an10-217-2-1

There are four kinds of corruption and failure of verbal action that have unskillful intention, with suffering as their outcome and result. ^an10-217-2-2

There are three kinds of corruption and failure of mental action that have unskillful intention, with suffering as their outcome and result. ^an10-217-2-3

And what are the three kinds of corruption and failure of bodily action? ^an10-217-3-1

It’s when a certain person kills living creatures. They’re violent, bloody-handed, a hardened killer, merciless to living beings. ^an10-217-3-2

They steal. With the intention to commit theft, they take the wealth or belongings of others from village or wilderness. ^an10-217-4-1

They commit sexual misconduct. They have sexual relations with women who have their mother, father, both mother and father, brother, sister, relatives, or clan as guardian. They have sexual relations with a woman who is protected on principle, or who has a husband, or whose violation is punishable by law, or even one who has been garlanded as a token of betrothal. ^an10-217-5-1

These are the three kinds of corruption and failure of bodily action. ^an10-217-6-1

And what are the four kinds of corruption and failure of verbal action? ^an10-217-7-1

It’s when a certain person lies. They’re summoned to a council, an assembly, a family meeting, a guild, or to the royal court, and asked to bear witness: ^an10-217-7-2

‘Please, my man, say what you know.’ Not knowing, they say ‘I know.’ Knowing, they say ‘I don’t know.’ Not seeing, they say ‘I see.’ And seeing, they say ‘I don’t see.’ So they deliberately lie for the sake of themselves or another, or for some trivial worldly reason. ^an10-217-7-3

They backbite. They repeat in one place what they heard in another so as to divide people against each other. And so they divide those who are harmonious, supporting division, delighting in division, loving division, speaking words that promote division. ^an10-217-8-1

They speak harshly. They use the kinds of words that are cruel, nasty, hurtful, offensive, bordering on anger, not leading to immersion. ^an10-217-9-1

They indulge in talking nonsense. Their speech is untimely, and is neither factual nor beneficial. It has nothing to do with the teaching or the training. Their words have no value, and are untimely, unreasonable, rambling, and pointless. ^an10-217-10-1

These are the four kinds of corruption and failure of verbal action. ^an10-217-11-1

And what are the three kinds of corruption and failure of mental action? ^an10-217-12-1

It’s when someone is covetous. They covet the wealth and belongings of others: ‘Oh, if only their belongings were mine!’ ^an10-217-12-2

They have ill will and malicious intentions: ‘May these sentient beings be killed, slaughtered, slain, destroyed, or annihilated!’ ^an10-217-13-1

They have wrong view. Their perspective is distorted: ^an10-217-14-1

‘There’s no meaning in giving, sacrifice, or offerings. There’s no fruit or result of good and bad deeds. There’s no afterlife. There’s no such thing as mother and father, or beings that are reborn spontaneously. And there’s no ascetic or brahmin who is rightly comported and rightly practiced, and who describes the afterlife after realizing it with their own insight.’ ^an10-217-14-2

These are the three kinds of corruption and failure of mental action. ^an10-217-15-1

When their body breaks up, after death, sentient beings are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell because of these three kinds of corruption and failure of bodily action, these four kinds of corruption and failure of verbal action, or these three kinds of corruption and failure of mental action that have unskillful intention, with suffering as their outcome and result. ^an10-217-16-1

It’s like throwing sure-bet dice: they always fall the right side up. ^an10-217-17-1

In the same way, when their body breaks up, after death, sentient beings are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell because of these three kinds of corruption and failure of bodily action, these four kinds of corruption and failure of verbal action, or these three kinds of corruption and failure of mental action that have unskillful intention, with suffering as their outcome and result. ^an10-217-17-2

I don’t say that intentional deeds that have been performed and accumulated are eliminated without being experienced. ^an10-217-18-1

And that may be in this very life, or in the next life, or in some subsequent period. ^an10-217-18-2

But in any case, there is no making an end of suffering without experiencing intentional deeds that have been performed and accumulated, I say. ^an10-217-18-3

Now, there are three kinds of successful bodily action that have skillful intention, with happiness as their outcome and result. ^an10-217-19-1

There are four kinds of successful verbal action that have skillful intention, with happiness as their outcome and result. ^an10-217-19-2

There are three kinds of successful mental action that have skillful intention, with happiness as their outcome and result. ^an10-217-19-3

And what are the three kinds of successful bodily action? ^an10-217-20-1

It’s when a certain person gives up killing living creatures. They renounce the rod and the sword. They’re scrupulous and kind, living full of sympathy for all living beings. ^an10-217-20-2

They don’t steal. They don’t, with the intention to commit theft, take the wealth or belongings of others from village or wilderness. ^an10-217-21-1

They give up sexual misconduct. They don’t have sexual relations with women who have their mother, father, both mother and father, brother, sister, relatives, or clan as guardian. They don’t have sexual relations with a woman who is protected on principle, or who has a husband, or whose violation is punishable by law, or even one who has been garlanded as a token of betrothal. ^an10-217-22-1

These are the three kinds of successful bodily action. ^an10-217-23-1

And what are the four kinds of successful verbal action? ^an10-217-24-1

It’s when a certain person gives up lying. They’re summoned to a council, an assembly, a family meeting, a guild, or to the royal court, and asked to bear witness: ‘Please, my man, say what you know.’ Not knowing, they say ‘I don’t know.’ Knowing, they say ‘I know.’ Not seeing, they say ‘I don’t see.’ And seeing, they say ‘I see.’ They don’t deliberately lie for the sake of themselves or another, or for some trivial worldly reason. ^an10-217-24-2

They give up backbiting. They don’t repeat in one place what they heard in another so as to divide people against each other. Instead, they reconcile those who are divided and support those who are united, delighting in harmony, loving harmony, speaking words that promote harmony. ^an10-217-25-1

They give up harsh speech. They speak in a way that’s mellow, pleasing to the ear, endearing, going to the heart, polite, likable and agreeable to the people. ^an10-217-26-1

They give up talking nonsense. Their words are timely, true, and meaningful, in line with the teaching and training. They say things at the right time which are valuable, reasonable, succinct, and beneficial. ^an10-217-27-1

These are the four kinds of successful verbal action. ^an10-217-28-1

And what are the three kinds of successful mental action? ^an10-217-29-1

It’s when someone is content. They don’t covet the wealth and belongings of others: ‘Oh, if only their belongings were mine!’ ^an10-217-29-2

They have a kind heart and loving intentions: ‘May these sentient beings live free of enmity and ill will, untroubled and happy!’ ^an10-217-30-1

They have right view, an undistorted perspective: ^an10-217-31-1

‘There is meaning in giving, sacrifice, and offerings. There are fruits and results of good and bad deeds. There is an afterlife. There are such things as mother and father, and beings that are reborn spontaneously. And there are ascetics and brahmins who are rightly comported and rightly practiced, and who describe the afterlife after realizing it with their own insight.’ ^an10-217-31-2

These are the three kinds of successful mental action. ^an10-217-32-1

When their body breaks up, after death, sentient beings are reborn in a good place, in heaven because of these three kinds of successful bodily action, these four kinds of successful verbal action, or these three kinds of successful mental action that have skillful intention, with happiness as their outcome and result. ^an10-217-33-1

It’s like throwing unfaling dice: they always fall the right side up. ^an10-217-34-1

In the same way, when their body breaks up, after death, sentient beings are reborn in a good place, in heaven because of these three kinds of successful bodily action, these four kinds of successful verbal action, or these three kinds of successful mental action that have skillful intention, with happiness as their outcome and result. ^an10-217-34-2

I don’t say that intentional deeds that have been performed and accumulated are eliminated without being experienced. ^an10-217-34-3

And that may be in this very life, or in the next life, or in some subsequent period. ^an10-217-34-4

But in any case, there is no making an end of suffering without experiencing intentional deeds that have been performed and accumulated, I say.” ^an10-217-34-5

### Intentional (2nd) (Numbered Discourses 10.218) ^an10-218-0

“Mendicants, I don’t say that intentional deeds that have been performed and accumulated are eliminated without being experienced. ^an10-218-1-1

And that may be in this very life, or in the next life, or in some subsequent period. ^an10-218-1-2

But in any case, there is no making an end of suffering without experiencing intentional deeds that have been performed and accumulated, I say. ^an10-218-1-3

Now, there are three kinds of corruption and failure of bodily action that have unskillful intention, with suffering as their outcome and result. ^an10-218-2-1

There are four kinds of corruption and failure of verbal action that have unskillful intention, with suffering as their outcome and result. ^an10-218-2-2

There are three kinds of corruption and failure of mental action that have unskillful intention, with suffering as their outcome and result. ^an10-218-2-3

And what are the three kinds of corruption and failure of bodily action? … ^an10-218-3-1

These are the three kinds of corruption and failure of bodily action. ^an10-218-3-2

And what are the four kinds of corruption and failure of verbal action? … ^an10-218-4-1

These are the four kinds of corruption and failure of verbal action. ^an10-218-4-2

And what are the three kinds of corruption and failure of mental action? … ^an10-218-5-1

These are the three kinds of corruption and failure of mental action. ^an10-218-5-2

When their body breaks up, after death, sentient beings are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell because of these three kinds of corruption and failure of bodily action, these four kinds of corruption and failure of verbal action, or these three kinds of corruption and failure of mental action that have unskillful intention, with suffering as their outcome and result. ^an10-218-6-1

I don’t say that intentional deeds that have been performed and accumulated are eliminated without being experienced. ^an10-218-7-1

And that may be in this very life, or in the next life, or in some subsequent period. ^an10-218-7-2

But in any case, there is no making an end of suffering without experiencing intentional deeds that have been performed and accumulated, I say. ^an10-218-7-3

Now, there are three kinds of successful bodily action that have skillful intention, with happiness as their outcome and result. ^an10-218-8-1

There are four kinds of successful verbal action that have skillful intention, with happiness as their outcome and result. ^an10-218-8-2

There are three kinds of successful mental action that have skillful intention, with happiness as their outcome and result. ^an10-218-8-3

And what are the three kinds of successful bodily action? … ^an10-218-9-1

These are the three kinds of successful bodily action. ^an10-218-9-2

And what are the four kinds of successful verbal action? … ^an10-218-10-1

These are the four kinds of successful verbal action. ^an10-218-10-2

And what are the three kinds of successful mental action? … ^an10-218-11-1

These are the three kinds of successful mental action. ^an10-218-11-2

When their body breaks up, after death, sentient beings are reborn in a good place, in heaven because of these three kinds of successful bodily action, these four kinds of successful verbal action, or these three kinds of successful mental action that have skillful intention, with happiness as their outcome and result. …” ^an10-218-12-1

### The Body Born of Deeds (Numbered Discourses 10.219) ^an10-219-0

“Mendicants, I don’t say that intentional deeds that have been performed and accumulated are eliminated without being experienced. ^an10-219-1-1

And that may be in this very life, or in the next life, or in some subsequent period. ^an10-219-1-2

But in any case, there is no making an end of suffering without experiencing intentional deeds that have been performed and accumulated, I say. ^an10-219-1-3

That noble disciple is rid of desire, rid of ill will, unconfused, aware, and mindful. They meditate spreading a heart full of love to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth. In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of love to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will. ^an10-219-2-1

They understand: ^an10-219-3-1

‘Formerly my mind was limited and undeveloped. Now it’s limitless and well developed. Whatever limited deeds I’ve done don’t remain or persist there.’ ^an10-219-3-2

What do you think, mendicants? ^an10-219-4-1

Suppose a child had developed the heart’s release by love from their childhood on. Would they still do any bad deed?” ^an10-219-4-2

“No, sir.” ^an10-219-4-3

“Not doing any bad deed, would they still experience any suffering?” ^an10-219-5-1

“No, sir. ^an10-219-5-2

For if they don’t do any bad deed, from where would suffering afflict them?” ^an10-219-5-3

“This heart’s release by love should be developed by women or men. ^an10-219-6-1

For neither women nor men take this body with them when they go. ^an10-219-6-2

The mind is what’s inside mortal beings. ^an10-219-6-3

They understand: ^an10-219-6-4

‘Whatever bad deeds I have done in the past with this deed-born body I will experience here. ^an10-219-6-5
